text: Highlight Clip Michael Saylor: Capital Could Flow Back Into Bitcoin by Year-End Strategy founder Michael Saylor said in a June 16 interview with Natalie Brunell that the market is in an "AI summer," with Wall Street promoting AI data center financing deals that are temporarily siphoning capital from Bitcoin. He said this is likely a 12 to 24 week rotation cycle. Once those AI deals close, enter lock-up periods, and early funds and traders cash out, capital will look for the next destination and eventually rotate back into Bitcoin. Saylor expects signs of Bitcoin capital inflows by year-end. — link

text: INTEL: CME GROUP TO SUE THE CFTC OVER ITS APPROVAL OF PERPETUAL FUTURES, CEO TERRENCE DUFFY SAYS, PER CNBC Duffy argues perpetual futures are legally swaps under Dodd-Frank, not ordinary futures and that CME holds exclusive licenses with the benchmark providers, so the products would have to route through CME regardless. Filing expected today. Follows the CFTC's late-May approval letting Kalshi and Coinbase offer regulated crypto perps in the US, the first time the product has been allowed onshore. CFTC chair Selig has defended the decision, saying incumbents "always fear the future." Twitter
author: admin#2457358877

text: Just In: CME to Sue CFTC Over U.S. Approval of Perpetual Futures CME Group, the world's leading derivatives marketplace, will sue the CFTC over its approval of perpetual futures in the U.S., outgoing CEO Terrence Duffy said. The dispute follows the CFTC's late-May approval allowing platforms including Kalshi and Coinbase to offer regulated crypto perpetual futures. Duffy argued that perpetual futures should be treated as swaps under the Dodd-Frank Act rather than ordinary futures, and said CME has exclusive rights to the relevant benchmark provider, meaning related products should go through CME regardless of whether they are structured as perpetual contracts. — link

text: Sources: ECB President Lagarde Opposed Binance's Entry Into EU Market; France May Be Its Last MiCA Option French crypto media outlet The Big Whale, citing sources familiar with the matter, reported that ECB President Christine Lagarde expressed opposition to Binance's entry into the EU market in discussions with the Greek government. Sources believe the intervention may have influenced Greece's stance on Binance's MiCA license application. Following reports that Greece's HCMC is preparing to reject Binance's MiCA application, France is said to have emerged as Binance's last viable option to retain access to the European market. Binance previously said it had submitted a comprehensive MiCA application to Greece's HCMC, which it believes has completed its review and found the application compliant with MiCA requirements. The company added that the application would also be subject to review at the ESMA level. — link

text: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady, In Line With Expectations The Federal Reserve left the federal funds rate target range unchanged at 3.50%-3.75% in a unanimous 12-0 vote. The FOMC said economic activity continues to expand at a solid pace, while inflation remains elevated relative to its 2% target. WSJ reporter Nick Timiraos said the Federal Reserve's latest dot plot was highly hawkish. Nine of 18 officials projected at least one rate hike this year, with six of them projecting multiple hikes. Only one official projected a rate cut, while one participant did not submit a Summary of Economic Projections (SEP). Timiraos added that the FOMC statement was rewritten from top to bottom and was much shorter than the previous version. — link

text: Mexican billionaire Ricardo Salinas Pliego, ranked seventh among Mexico's richest, said around 70% of his investment portfolio is allocated to Bitcoin, arguing that fiat currencies will lose purchasing power over time. Salinas said he once advised his wife to mortgage her home and borrow to buy Bitcoin, and said people should consider converting part of their home equity into Bitcoin exposure. He added that Bitcoin has outperformed traditional stores of value such as real estate over the long term and said Bitcoin will reach $1 million, though he does not know when. — link

text: QCP Market Colour – 17 June 2026 Markets have been given a cleaner geopolitical setup to start the week. An MOU between the US and Iran has signalled a potential end to the conflict, with the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz giving risk assets room to breathe. S&P futures opened more than 100 points above Friday's close and pushed past all-time highs, while crude moved lower and is now trading below 75. The macro backdrop is still doing some heavy lifting. Warsh takes the stage at his first Fed meeting as Chair today, facing elevated inflation, a divided Board, and a market already pricing in 0.5 hikes in 2026. The Dot Plot will be the key test of whether policy is set to stay restrictive in the near term. Equities, meanwhile, have found another source of momentum. SpaceX's blockbuster launch has added fuel to an already strong tape, with AI and space now both helping to keep risk appetite alive. Low float and strong institutional demand may keep the stock supported, though the rally is starting to look rather gravity-resistant. For crypto, the picture is less clean. BTC remains stuck below 66k as concerns around Strategy's funding needs continue to weigh on sentiment. Macro optimism may eventually catch up, but for now, the Strategy overhang is keeping Bitcoin from joining the broader risk rally. Is Bitcoin simply lagging the macro move, or does this overhang need to clear before the next leg higher? Read the full colour here.

text: Ethereum's Glamsterdam Upgrade: The Biggest Planned Upgrade for 2026 Ethereum core developers have entered the final stretch for the next major network upgrade, Glamsterdam. Teams have begun full-scale testing of a fork incorporating all planned Ethereum Improvement Proposals (EIPs) on private developer networks (devnets), marking the final development phase before the codebase is frozen and deployed to public testnets. This upgrade focuses on supercharging Layer 1 scaling. It introduces key improvements like Enshrined Proposer-Builder Separation (ePBS) and Block-Level Access Lists. — link

text: RL Systems Mind the Gap: Matching Trainer and Generator Throughput Reinforcement learning (RL) training system efficiency dictates how far model capabilities can scale, and this efficiency ultimately relies on successfully matching the throughput of the generator and the trainer to prevent infrastructure starvation or excessive policy staleness Modern RL runs are heavily generation-bound due to long model response traces, intensive tool-use verification, and sandbox scaling bottlenecks that leave the trainer idling for a significant percentage of compute time

text: Variational and the Shift to Onchain Brokerage Traditional CLOB DEXs struggle to scale beyond highly liquid crypto assets because rebuilding deep order books for RWAs is slow and capital-intensive Variational addresses this by using a RFQ brokerage model that aggregates existing global liquidity rather than trying to recreate it, enabling the platform to list 469 perpetual markets without needing passive liquidity on day one Instead of letting value leak to external market makers through spreads, Variational routes trades through its own OLP layer, which acts as the direct counterparty to trades while actively hedging net risk across external venues. This system keeps spread revenue inside the protocol (recycling a portion into the treasury and user yields) while utilizing isolated onchain settlement pools to prevent counterparties from impacting the broader ecosystem
author: admin#1742094092

text: The Next AI Bottleneck Isn't Intelligence. It's Money. As AI mature, the primary constraint for enterprises has shifted from technological capability to finite corporate budgets, turning AI deployment into a strict capital-allocation decision Consequently, market demand is migrating away from expensive frontier models toward "good enough," cheap, and reliable intelligence that offers predictable costs and acceptable performance
author: admin#1742094092

text: NYM: SBF Says He May Launch a New Token After Prison According to New York Magazine, new details have emerged about the prison life of Sam Bankman-Fried (SBF), the founder of FTX who is serving a 25-year prison sentence. Now 34, SBF reportedly takes Adderall daily for clinical depression and ADHD and has been writing a serialized prison memoir titled Manfred. When asked by fellow inmate David Bunevacz about his plans after release, SBF said that making serious money would require an initial $50 million to $100 million to build a real business, adding that he would "start my own coin" after leaving prison and that "everyone will flock to it." Bunevacz later remarked, "He may have been joking, and they probably won't flock to it. But who knows." SBF formally submitted a presidential pardon application to the Trump administration on June 8. Following the news, the probability of a pardon being granted on Polymarket doubled to 14%. — link
